hypothesis: For 4099 deterministically generated binary traces of length 64, the CUDA sideways reconstruction returns all 63 reconstructed initial-left bits per trace exactly as an independent CPU oracle; timings determine relative performance for this exact one-chunk workload.
interpretation: All 258237 finite output bytes matched the independent CPU oracle in the warm-up and every measured run. CUDA's 3.946667 ms median end-to-end time was slightly below the CPU oracle's 4.131626 ms median for this exact one-chunk workload, a measured ratio of 1.046865 that must not be generalized.
limitations: The test covers 4099 finite pseudorandom traces through reconstruction horizon 63; it does not establish compatibility or incompatibility of any infinite or eventually periodic center trace.
The small measured CUDA advantage applies only to this exact one-chunk workload and this host; it is not a general GPU speed claim.
The CPU oracle is an independent single-threaded O(horizon)-workspace implementation of the same finite mapping.
One CPU warm-up and one GPU warm-up preceded five measured repetitions; frequency scaling and unrelated host activity were not disabled.
The candidate count requires a partial final CUDA block, but the entire batch fit in one device-memory chunk; this record does not measure chunk overhead.
Sideways reconstruction performs quadratic work in the finite horizon and supplies no asymptotic shortcut for c_n.
The 39 C benchmark-session readings and 40 C final idle reading are coarse snapshots, not a per-repetition thermal trace; no clock, voltage, fan, power, or thermal control was changed.
Compute Sanitizer 2026.2.1 could not initialize its WSL/WDDM debugger interface, so this milestone has passing functional tests but no successful Compute Sanitizer run.

parameters.trace_generation: xorshift with shifts 13, 7, 17; each low bit is emitted

proof_scope: Exact finite equality of all 4099*63 reconstructed bytes between the independent CPU oracle and CUDA in the warm-up and each of five measured runs; all performance conclusions are empirical and workload-specific.
question: problem1

software.algorithm: one CUDA thread per trace; finite Rule 30 left-permutive sideways reconstruction with three horizon-plus-two-byte work buffers per candidate
